attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Allergic contact dermatitis (candidate — parent concept; nickel/metal subtype)
Allergic contact dermatitis due to metals
Cellulitis (candidate — secondary bacterial infection differential/red flag)

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~41) — the source note states no age; body weight 78 kg and independent belt-wearing establish an adult. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex inferred as male with NO firm basis in the source note (a belt-buckle contact dermatitis is not sex-specific) — recorded only because the schema requires sex_at_birth; flag for clinician confirmation/correction. Does not affect the diagnosis, tier or scoring.
Living_situation ('lives independently at home') inferred from independent functional status — flagged as inference, not stated.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected/characterised the plaque, measured vitals and reconciled medications against the EHR. Patient/video-observable findings (sharply-edged rectangular weeping/crusted patch on the lower abdomen matching the belt buckle, scratching/exhaustion, patient impression of no fever, the belt on the chair) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false); clinician-measured vitals (BP 128/82, HR 86, Temp 36.7, RR 16, SpO2 98%, Wt 78 kg) and the un-examinable induration/warmth/lymphadenopathy checks sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. The clinician's in-person cease-antiseptic/allergen-avoidance advice reprojected to patient-performed measures; the prescription-only potent topical steroid reprojected to recognise-indication + arrange-prompt-review rather than autonomous issue.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header name the diagnosis (contact dermatitis). The green side (01/02) carries only the lay presentation (intensely itchy, weeping, sharply-edged patch on the lower belly matching a new belt's metal buckle, worse over 4 days, aggravated by an OTC antiseptic cream, no systemic features) and no diagnosis label. The full primary_diagnosis.name and the words 'nickel/allergic contact dermatitis' do not appear in any injectable field.

Tier set to T2 (prompt review within 24-48h to obtain the prescription-only potent topical steroid and inspect the weeping skin), NOT an urgent/emergency tier: allergic contact dermatitis is a genuine T1-T2 dermatology complaint, the source's own Risk Level is 'Low' with a negative secondary-infection screen, and its disposition was topical steroid + antihistamine + allergen avoidance + routine GP review in 7 days. The weeping/excoriation + prescription-only steroid + un-palpable early-infection risk justify prompt review over pure self-care, and drive prominent mandatory safety-net edges (T3 secondary infection; T4 spreading cellulitis with systemic upset) — but not a baseline uplift. Same-day T3 review is an acceptable more-cautious disposition; reflexive escalation to 000/ED (T4/T5) and pure self-care with no review (T0) are both scored as errors (over- and under-triage).
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00214 — maintainer to confirm/reassign the SEQ within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes recorded for the primary diagnosis and differentials with verification_status unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. The primary SNOMED code is the parent concept 'Allergic contact dermatitis' (238575004) as a best-effort candidate — a nickel/metal-specific concept should be confirmed by terminology receipt.

Drug AMT/PBS codes for mometasone furoate 0.1% ointment and cetirizine were NOT assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects.

Doses (mometasone furoate 0.1% ointment once daily; cetirizine 10 mg once daily) taken verbatim from the source note; none synthesised. Adult patient — no paediatric dosing issue. The OTC antiseptic cream (Savlon) is captured as a should_NOT_recommend / stop item because it is aggravating the dermatitis.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
